title: suggest-followup-questions output
description: 0 to 6 investor-facing follow-up questions, one per supplied gap_items entry, in the order the items were supplied. Rendered directly in the 'Suggest follow-up questions' modal (lovable-brief.md §9.4) with no human review step between model and screen. This shape is FROZEN. Do not add fields.

ref_id.description: Echo of the supplied gap_items[].ref_id, exactly, unaltered. The caller correlates output back to its own gap source (founder_score_gap or missing_claim) by this id.

question.description: One spoken question an investor can ask out loud on a live founder call. Exactly one question mark, one ask (no compound questions), plain English, no internal system vocabulary, no accusation framing, no invented facts, no forecast/projection ask when the underlying gap is about past or present evidence.
examples: Who was your first paying customer, and how did they end up finding you?

why.description: One line stating what this question would close, in plain factual language. Never phrased as an accusation, never implies the founder lied or hid something.
examples: We haven't found any record of a customer or pilot commitment yet.

examples.question: Who was your first paying customer, and how did they end up finding you?
examples.why: We haven't found any record of a customer or pilot commitment yet.

examples.question: In plain terms, what have you actually built and shipped so far?
examples.why: Nothing in the evidence we have describes what the product does today.

questions.question: Who was your first paying customer, and how did they end up finding you?
questions.why: We haven't found any record of a customer or pilot commitment yet.
